name: Richard Belzer
blurb: He served as the warm-up comedian for Saturday Night Live and recorded the HBO special Another Lone Nut, though he became most widely known for playing Detective John Munch across more than two decades of television.
bio: Richard Belzer was an American stand-up comedian, actor, and author known for his caustic, deadpan observational style honed at New York clubs like Catch a Rising Star in the 1970s. He served as the warm-up comedian for Saturday Night Live and recorded the HBO special Another Lone Nut, though he became most widely known for playing Detective John Munch across more than two decades of television.

timeline.ev: Begins stand-up career in New York City
Played multiple roles in cult film The Groove Tube
Began playing Det. John Munch on Homicide: Life on the Street
HBO stand-up special Another Lone Nut
Began 15-season run as Munch on Law & Order: SVU
Died February 19 in Bozouls, France, aged 78

accolades: Author of How to Be a Stand-Up Comic
Played Det. John Munch on Law & Order: SVU (1999-2016) — the role spanned 22 consecutive primetime seasons across Homicide and SVU
Munch appeared on a record 11 different TV shows played by a single actor

movies_tv.t: Lois & Clark: The New Adventures of Superman

bits.t: Belzer and Jack Nicholson at Burger King
Belzer and Jack Nicholson at Burger King

flags.bits_honest_gap_note: Honest gap: only one embeddable official clip found. Belzer (d.2023) has just two videos on his official channel (TheOfficialBelzer), neither stand-up (a music video and a talk-show bit). His 1997 special Another Lone Nut survives on streaming audio (Spotify/Apple) but has no auto-generated 'Richard Belzer - Topic' YouTube channel. His Letterman appearances on YouTube are mostly the Don Giller fan-archive recut (rejected as a re-rip); the official @Letterman channel surfaces only the single clip below (his 1982 Late Night debut; logged venue kept as the Letterman TV entry). All other stand-up clips were fan uploads.

bits.yt_title: If Richard Belzer And Jack Nicholson Went To Burger King | Letterman
